Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your field service system is secure, accessible, and connected to your other business tools. Singapore SMEs must comply with PDPA data protection rules, and your technicians need to work reliably on phones and tablets in the field. When your dispatcher integrates customer data from your accounting system or exports job records for invoicing, this family makes it seamless and safe.

Work Order Core.narrative: This is the heartbeat of field service—creating jobs, sending technicians to fix them, and capturing proof of completion. SMEs need to track every service visit from start to finish, ensure customers sign off on work, and maintain compliance records (especially for regulated industries like HVAC or electrical). Without this, you lose visibility into what your team is doing and can't prove the work was done correctly.

Scheduling & Dispatch.narrative: This family gets the right technician to the right job at the right time, saving fuel costs and improving customer satisfaction. Singapore SMEs with multiple field staff need to balance workload fairly, avoid wasted travel time, and know where technicians are in real time. When you have five jobs across the island and only two technicians, smart scheduling means you complete more jobs per day and reduce overtime.

Equipment & Inventory.narrative: This family tracks what you service, what parts you have, and when maintenance is due—preventing stock-outs and missed service windows. SMEs that service installed equipment (air-con units, generators, lifts) need to know warranty status before quoting, manage van stock so technicians don't return empty-handed, and schedule preventive maintenance to keep customers happy. Without it, you either over-stock parts or leave technicians stranded.

Service Contracts.narrative: This family manages what you've promised to customers—response times, warranty coverage, and pricing—and turns completed work into invoices automatically. SMEs with service contracts need to enforce SLAs (e.g., 4-hour response for critical breakdowns), avoid billing disputes by quoting upfront, and let customers track their own requests online. It also builds trust through transparent communication and reduces admin overhead.

Analytics & Intelligence.narrative: This family shows you whether your field service business is actually profitable and where bottlenecks are. SMEs need to see metrics like first-time fix rate (are technicians solving problems on the first visit?) and technician utilisation (are your staff busy or idle?) to make smarter hiring and pricing decisions. When you know that Job X cost $500 in labour and parts but only earned $400, you can fix your pricing or process.
